name: Tyler — Nextcloud Behind Cloudflare Tunnel 502 Error
topic: Nextcloud accessible locally but returns 502 Bad Gateway through Cloudflare Tunnel

turns.content: I set up Nextcloud in Docker on my Ubuntu server and it works fine at 192.168.1.50:8080, but when I try to access it through my Cloudflare Tunnel at cloud.mydomain.com I get a 502 Bad Gateway error.

Classic tunnel-to-container issue. What Ubuntu version are you on, and is cloudflared running on the same host as Docker or separately? What's the tunnel config pointing to — localhost:8080 or the host IP?

Ubuntu 24.04 LTS server. cloudflared is running as a systemd service on the same host as Docker. The tunnel is pointing to http://localhost:8080. I set it up through the Cloudflare Zero Trust dashboard.

Good — Ubuntu 24.04, same host. Check two things. First, SSL mode in Cloudflare dashboard — set it to Full, not Full (Strict). Second, verify cloudflared can reach Nextcloud: run `curl -I http://localhost:8080` on the server. What do you get?

curl gives me HTTP/1.1 200 OK with some Nextcloud headers. SSL mode was set to Full (Strict) which I changed to Full in the Cloudflare dashboard. Waited a couple minutes but still getting 502 Bad Gateway in the browser.

SSL change can take a minute to propagate. Also check: in your Nextcloud config.php, is your tunnel domain in the trusted_domains array? Without it, Nextcloud rejects the request. Find it at your Nextcloud volume path, usually something like `docker exec nextcloud cat /var/www/html/config/config.php | grep trusted`.

Found it — the trusted_domains array in config.php only has localhost and 192.168.1.50 listed. The tunnel domain cloud.mydomain.com is not in there. How do I add it without breaking the existing entries?

Run: `docker exec -u www-data nextcloud php occ config:system:set trusted_domains 2 --value=cloud.mydomain.com`. Then test cloud.mydomain.com in your browser. You should see the Nextcloud login. If it still fails, check `docker logs nextcloud --tail 20` for errors. To revert, run the same command with `--value=` to clear it.
That fixed it! Nextcloud loads through the tunnel now.

Good. Verify: open an incognito window to cloud.mydomain.com and confirm login works. Then enable WebSockets in your tunnel settings (Zero Trust > tunnel > public hostname > Connection) — Nextcloud uses them for real-time file updates. Verify WebSockets by uploading a file and confirming it appears in real time on another device. Set a strong admin password if you haven't — this is now internet-facing.
